identity.short_name: Centered symmetric quartic no-real-root count -> cubic power-sum partial sum

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count monic quartics symmetric about 1 with no real roots); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Partial sum of cubic-root power sums modulo 1000) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Count (a,b) in a centered-even quartic y^4+ay^2+b with positivity on y^2>=0 to get T, then use T as the endpoint of a fixed cubic-root power-sum recurrence sum modulo 1000.
givens: T counts pairs (a,b) for which P_{a,b} has no real roots.
alpha,beta,gamma are roots of y^3-3y+1=0; S_n=alpha^n+beta^n+gamma^n.

parameter(s).name: coeff_bounds
parameter(s).purpose: finite box for the quartic count

parameter(s).name: degree
parameter(s).purpose: fixes quartic setting (even-after-shift reduction to y^4+by^2+d)

parameter(s).name: symmetry_center
parameter(s).purpose: determines the translation y=x-1 that makes the polynomial even

parameter(s).name: leading_coefficient
parameter(s).purpose: monic quartic so end behavior is +infinity and "no real roots implies strict positivity

parameter(s).name: coeff_bound
parameter(s).purpose: bounds integer coefficients to make the admissible set finite

parameter(s).name: cubic_coefficients
parameter(s).domain: monic cubic x^3 - s1 x^2 + s2 x - s3 with integer coefficients
parameter(s).purpose: determines the symmetric sums and the induced power-sum recurrence

parameter(s).name: m
parameter(s).purpose: modulus for the final reduction (here m=1000)

parameter(s).name: output_statistic
parameter(s).purpose: output is T=_{n=0}^N S_n rather than a single S_N

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: even_shift_invariant
invariant(s).statement: After shifting by x->y+1, the quartic becomes y^4+ay^2+b (even).

invariant(s).name: positivity_on_halfline
invariant(s).statement: No real roots iff z^2+az+b>0 for all z>=0 with z=y^2.

invariant(s).name: symmetry_implies_even_after_shift
invariant(s).statement: Let y=x-1 and Q(y)=P(y+1). Then Q is even: Q(-y)=Q(y), so Q(y)=y^4+by^2+d.

invariant(s).name: coefficient_relations_from_center_one_symmetry
invariant(s).statement: For monic quartics with P(2-x)=P(x), necessarily a_3=-4 and a_1=8-2a_2. Equivalently P(x)=(x-1)^4+b(x-1)^2+d with integers b=a_2-6 and d=a_0-a_2+5.

invariant(s).name: positivity_reduction_to_quadratic
invariant(s).statement: With Q(y)=y^4+by^2+d, "no real roots (monic quartic) is equivalent to Q(y)>0 for all y, which is equivalent to z^2+bz+d>0 for all z>=0 via z=y^2.

invariant(s).name: partial_sum_determinism
invariant(s).statement: Once S_0,,S_N are determined, T=_{n=0}^N S_n is uniquely determined.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Strict inequality is used so boundary cases (zero) are excluded, guaranteeing no real roots.
Coefficients are integers and the polynomial is monic of degree 4.
"No real roots" is interpreted as P(x)0 for all real x; for monic quartics this is equivalent to strict positivity.
Integer coefficient bounds ensure finiteness of the search/count.
The polynomial is monic of degree 3, so every root satisfies the same cubic relation, yielding a linear recurrence for S_n.
Roots are counted with multiplicity (standard for Vieta/Newton sums).
Modular reduction is applied to integer values obtained from the recurrence.
The bridge quantity T computed from component A is guaranteed to be an integer in the admissible range required by component B (e.g., as an index, bound, or group-action parameter).
uniqueness_certificate(s): The finite case split on a gives a unique integer T.
Given T, the recurrence uniquely determines the partial sum modulo 1000.

Symmetry about x=1 forces the unique representation P(x)=(x-1)^4+b(x-1)^2+d with integers b,d. The coefficient bounds translate to a finite set of integer pairs (b,d). The condition "no real roots is equivalent to a strict quadratic inequality z^2+bz+d>0 on z>=0, which yields necessary-and-sufficient inequalities in (b,d). Therefore the admissible set is a uniquely determined finite set of lattice points, so the final count is unique.
The recurrence S_n = 3S_{n-2} - S_{n-3} together with initial values S_0=3, S_1=0, S_2=6 uniquely determines S_0,,S_20, hence uniquely determines T and T mod 1000.
(N1 necessity) If T is not computed from component A, component B has multiple admissible outputs across different possible parameter choices; thus the composite is ill-posed without the bridge.
(N2 non-factorization) The final answer is not a trivial Combine(ansA, ansB): the computed T restricts/changes the admissible branch structure inside component B before its output is even defined.

technique(s).name: even_quartic_to_quadratic_on_z_ge_0
technique(s).purpose: reduce no-real-root condition to positivity of quadratic on z>=0

technique(s).name: recurrence_sum_mod_1000
technique(s).purpose: compute partial sum to index T modulo 1000

technique(s).name: center_shift_to_even_polynomial
technique(s).purpose: turn functional symmetry into evenness after the shift y=x-1

technique(s).name: coefficient_comparison_and_parameterization
technique(s).purpose: derive forced relations among coefficients and identify integer parameters b,d
outputs: a_3=-4, a_1=8-2a_2; integers b,d determine P uniquely

technique(s).name: translate_bounds_to_integer_constraints
technique(s).purpose: reduce the search space to a finite set of integer pairs (b,d)
outputs: explicit ranges for b and d

technique(s).name: reduce_quartic_positivity_to_quadratic_on_z_ge_0
technique(s).purpose: reduce strict positivity of an even quartic to positivity of a quadratic on z>=0

technique(s).name: vertex_casework_for_quadratic_on_halfline
technique(s).purpose: derive necessary and sufficient positivity inequalities by comparing boundary vs vertex minimum
outputs: if b>=0 then d>0; if b<0 then d>b^2/4

technique(s).name: integer_threshold_counting
technique(s).purpose: count integer lattice points satisfying the constraints

technique(s).name: vieta_formulas_to_get_initial_power_sums
technique(s).purpose: compute S_0,S_1,S_2 from the elementary symmetric sums

technique(s).name: linear_recurrence_from_root_equation
technique(s).purpose: derive a constant-coefficient recurrence for S_n by multiplying r^3=3r-1 by r^{n-3} and summing

technique(s).name: iterative_recurrence_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: compute S_0,,S_N (optionally reducing mod m along the way)

technique(s).name: partial_sum_modular_accumulation
technique(s).purpose: compute T=_{n=0}^N S_n and reduce modulo m

technique(s).name: modular_reduction_final
technique(s).purpose: present the final residue class representative in {0,1,,m1}

theorem(s).name: vertex_minimum_for_quadratic
theorem(s).role: minimum on halfline occurs at boundary or vertex

theorem(s).name: even_function_polynomial_form
theorem(s).role: an even quartic polynomial has only even powers (y^4, y^2, constant)

theorem(s).name: quadratic_minimum_at_vertex
theorem(s).role: the minimum of z^2+bz+d occurs at z=-b/2, governing positivity on an interval/halfline

theorem(s).name: intermediate_value_principle_for_polynomials
theorem(s).role: for monic quartics, absence of real roots implies strict positivity everywhere

theorem(s).name: Vieta_formulas
theorem(s).role: relates coefficients to elementary symmetric polynomials of the roots

theorem(s).name: Newton_sums_identities
theorem(s).role: justifies deriving linear recurrences for power sums from the polynomial satisfied by the roots

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: symmetry reduction via change of variables
Linear dependence of powers of a root modulo its defining polynomial induces a recurrence for symmetric power sums.

critical_trick(s): Shift to make the quartic even and reduce to a quadratic on z=y^2>=0.
Count admissible (a,b) by a>=0 vs a<0 minimum-location case split.
Bridge the count T into the endpoint of the recurrence partial sum.
Bridge Lemma (explicit): compute T in component A, then substitute this specific T into component B; justify N1 and N2 to rule out stapling.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: basic modular arithmetic and combinatorial counts

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Execute the intended structural route in a CAS-friendly way: compute bridge T from component A using symbolic identities or explicit enumeration, then compute component B output with the substituted T, finally reduce mod 1000; confirm matches the recorded Answer.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent cross-check: avoid reusing the same derivation as primary. Use a different route (e.g., brute-force enumeration over the finite search space, numeric root approximation with direct evaluation, or an alternate invariant/Burnside count) to recompute the final answer and compare.
